text: Fill in the blanks about the general features of muscle.
questions: The three types of muscle tissue are *skeletal*, *cardiac*, and *smooth*.
Skeletal muscle is identified histologically by the presence of *striations* and multiple *peripheral* nuclei.
A broad flat tendon is called an *aponeurosis*, while a round cord-like attachment is a *tendon*.
A *spurt* muscle has its insertion far from the joint and is most effective as a prime mover, while a *shunt* muscle has its insertion close to the joint and stabilises it.
Cardiac muscle cells are connected by specialised junctions called *intercalated discs* that allow coordinated contraction.
